examples.title: Binary Resolution Algorithm
examples.description: Basic resolution rule for two clauses in propositional logic
examples.code:
FUNCTION Resolve(C1, C2):
 FOR each literal L1 in C1:
 FOR each literal L2 in C2:
 IF L1 = complement(L2):
 // Found complementary pair
 Resolvent = (C1 - {L1}) ∪ (C2 - {L2})
 RETURN Resolvent
 RETURN null // No resolvent possible

FUNCTION complement(L):
 IF L is positive: RETURN negation(L)
 ELSE: RETURN positive_form(L)
examples.explanation: Binary resolution checks all pairs of complementary literals between two clauses. When found, it removes them and combines remaining literals to form resolvent. Returns null if no resolution possible.

examples.title: Unification Algorithm
examples.description: Computes MGU for two literals in first-order logic
examples.code:
FUNCTION Unify(L1, L2, θ):
 IF θ is failure: RETURN failure
 IF L1 = L2: RETURN θ
 IF L1 is variable:
 RETURN UnifyVar(L1, L2, θ)
 IF L2 is variable:
 RETURN UnifyVar(L2, L1, θ)
 IF L1 or L2 are compound terms:
 IF functor(L1) ≠ functor(L2): RETURN failure
 RETURN Unify(args(L1), args(L2), θ)
 RETURN failure

FUNCTION UnifyVar(var, term, θ):
 IF var in θ: RETURN Unify(substitute(var, θ), term, θ)
 IF var occurs in term: RETURN failure // Occurs check
 RETURN compose(θ, {var/term})
examples.explanation: Recursive unification algorithm with occurs check. Handles variables, constants, and compound terms. Returns MGU (substitution) or failure if unification impossible.

examples.title: Resolution Refutation Prover
examples.description: Main algorithm for automated theorem proving using resolution
examples.code:
FUNCTION ResolutionRefutation(KB, Theorem):
 // Step 1: Convert to CNF
 CNF_KB = ToCNF(KB)
 Negated = ToCNF(NEGATE(Theorem))
 Clauses = CNF_KB ∪ {Negated}
 
 // Step 2: Resolution loop
 NEW = true
 WHILE NEW:
 NEW = false
 NEW_CLAUSES = []
 
 FOR each pair (Ci, Cj) in Clauses:
 Resolvent = Resolve(Ci, Cj)
 
 IF Resolvent = empty_clause:
 RETURN PROOF_FOUND // Contradiction!
 
 IF Resolvent is not subsumed and Resolvent not in Clauses:
 NEW_CLAUSES.append(Resolvent)
 NEW = true
 
 Clauses = Clauses ∪ NEW_CLAUSES
 
 RETURN NO_PROOF // Could not derive contradiction
examples.explanation: Main resolution refutation algorithm. Converts KB and negated theorem to CNF, then iteratively resolves all clause pairs until empty clause found (proving theorem) or exhausts possibilities (cannot prove).

examples.title: Horn Clause Resolution (SLD Resolution)
examples.description: Logic programming resolution strategy used by Prolog
examples.explanation: SLD (Selective Linear Definite) resolution is the foundation of Prolog. It uses definite Horn clauses and performs goal-reduction through backtracking. Queries are proved by finding matching facts or rules recursively.
examples.timeComplexity: Linear in proof depth (for fixed KB)

examples.title: CNF Conversion Algorithm
examples.description: Complete procedure to convert first-order formula to clause form
examples.explanation: Complete CNF conversion for first-order logic. Each step transforms the formula while preserving satisfiability. The final clause set is suitable for resolution-based theorem proving.
